methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Thoron is a seed-stage company operating in the Drones & Autonomous sector, founded by Thayne Kollmorgen. The broader sector includes eight private companies at various stages, anchored by peers such as Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air. No public comparables exist in this narrow category.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. The Drones & Autonomous sector contains only five same-sector peers total, and none satisfy the screening criteria for sufficient statistical rigor—zero companies matched on both stage and valuation tier, zero on adjacent stage, and zero on similar-tier benchmarks. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of company maturity, capital intensity, and business model within the broader universe.

Consequently, this analysis cannot triangulate a defensible valuation range using the available quantitative methods. A credible valuation of Thoron would require either: (1) expansion of the peer set to include earlier-stage or adjacent subsectors within autonomous systems; (2) forward-looking revenue or unit economics modeling specific to the company's differentiated position; or (3) benchmarking against seed-stage venture capital deployment patterns in adjacent hard-tech sectors. The current dataset supports no quantitative synthesis.
